concerns.analysis: The source PDF contains numerous meaningful images, including a collage of photographs and the NC State University logo on the cover page, as well as several charts and maps throughout the document. According to the image manifest, almost all embedded images (including maps for Figures 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, and 12) have corrupt base64 data ("valid": false) due to a missing Python module ('PIL'), meaning they will appear broken when rendered in a browser. Additionally, the charts for Figure 7 (page
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The source PDF contains meaningful maps and charts, such as the 'WATER WILLOW' map for Figure 3 (page 11) and the bar/line chart for Figure 7 (page 15). The first page also contains a collage of photos and an NC State University logo.
concerns.output_quote: The image manifest indicates the map for Figure 3 (and most other maps) is corrupt ('valid': false) and will not render. The HTML image for Figure 7 has alt text describing a page footer line, completely missing the actual chart. The cover page photos and university logo are missing from the HTML.

concerns.description: Table caption coded as header row: 2 table(s) have a single spanning first row that is likely a caption/title
concerns.analysis: caption_as_header=2. Examples: p8: "LAKE GASTON AQUATIC PLANT COMMUNITY - 2023"; p9: "LAKE GASTON HYDRILLA TUBER BANK ESTIMATES". First row spans all columns — should be a <caption> element, not a header row.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (38): Lake Gaston Aquatic Vegetation Report 2023; 1 | P a g e; Lake Gaston Aquatic Vegetation Report 2023
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=38. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

The source document uses underlining for specific labels on the title page ('Prepared By:' and 'Submitted To:'). In the JSON output, these strings are preserved as paragraphs with italics (using markdown asterisks), but the underline styling is entirely lost.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 2 table(s) with 3+ rows have no <th> header cells
concerns.analysis: data_in_th_time=0, no_th_tables=2. Minor semantic markup issues: tables lack <th> elements or use time values as headers. These affect screen reader context but are less severe than data misrepresentation.
